records.ety: [L. sesamum, sesama, Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/, <?/<?/<?/: cf. F. ssame.]

defs: Either of two annual herbaceous plants of the genus Sesamum (Sesamum Indicum, and Sesamum orientale), from the seeds of which an oil is expressed; also, the small obovate, flattish seeds of these plants, sometimes used as food. See Benne.
